# initServerConfigs: preserve operator edits when materializing stale server config imports

## Context

Surfaced during cycle-1 review of [PR #12044](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12044) (export-drift guard for `TenantRepoSyncService.resolveIngestionService`). Any unit test that calls a function which transitively `await import('ai/services.mjs')` in an operator-overlay context (where `ai/config.mjs` has already registered `Neo.ai.Config` extending the template) trips the `Neo.ai.Config` namespace-collision guard at [`src/Neo.mjs:820`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/src/Neo.mjs#L820):

```
Error: Namespace collision in unitTestMode for Neo.ai.Config
   at src/Neo.mjs:821
   at ai/config.template.mjs:578
```

PR #12044 worked around this with a per-test `Neo.config.unitTestMode = false` flip wrapped in `try/finally`. The flip is correct for the local case (the guard's non-strict branch is *exactly* designed for "different versions of Neo.mjs" / "different environments in parallel" — the same semantic that operator-overlay + template represents), but the workaround being needed at all is a substrate friction signal.

## The Problem

`unitTestMode` strict behavior treats namespace presence as the collision criterion:

```javascript
// src/Neo.mjs:819-825
if (ns) {
    if (Neo.config.unitTestMode) {
        throw new Error('Namespace collision in unitTestMode for ' + proto.constructor.config.className)
    }
    return ns
}
```

For operator-overlay-extending-template patterns (e.g., `Neo.ai.Config` registered first by `ai/config.mjs`, then `ai/config.template.mjs:578` re-attempts registration via a transitive import chain), the existing namespace is the *same class* — strict-mode treats it as a collision, but it's structurally a no-op re-registration.

The current strict-mode guard can't distinguish:
- **Real collision**: two different classes claiming the same `className` (bug — should error).
- **Self-equal re-registration**: same class registered twice through different import chains (operator-overlay + template; no-op intended).

## The Architectural Reality

- [`src/Neo.mjs:811-825`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/src/Neo.mjs#L811-L825) — `setupClass` namespace-collision guard. Comment at lines 811-818 explicitly names "different versions of Neo.mjs" and "different environments in parallel" as cases the non-strict branch was written for.
- [`ai/config.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/config.template.mjs) (gitignored operator overlay, generated from `ai/config.template.mjs`) — both register `Neo.ai.Config`.
- Any test that transitively triggers a re-import of either fails-loud under `unitTestMode: true`.

## The Fix (design discussion needed before mechanical PR)

Three options, listed in increasing intrusiveness:

### Option A — Class-identity-aware guard

Change the collision criterion from "namespace exists" to "namespace exists AND points to a different class identity":

```javascript
if (ns) {
    if (Neo.config.unitTestMode && ns !== proto.constructor) {
        // existing namespace points to a DIFFERENT class — real collision
        throw new Error('Namespace collision in unitTestMode for ' + ...);
    }
    return ns
}
```

**Tradeoff:** preserves strict-mode's intent (catch real collisions) while allowing self-equal re-registration. Backward-compat for existing strict-mode tests (real collisions still throw). Risk: subtle class-identity comparison semantics under module re-loads (Playwright workers, hot-reload, etc.).

### Option B — Test infra: don't auto-load operator overlay in test mode

Have the test runner (or `setup.mjs`) detect `unitTestMode: true` and bypass the operator overlay loading entirely — tests always run against canonical template. Pure-template test environment.

**Tradeoff:** keeps strict-mode semantics intact. But blocks tests that *need* to exercise the overlay path (e.g., tests verifying operator-specific config behavior). Some tests would need a "load overlay" opt-in.

### Option C — Document the per-test flip pattern as canonical

Treat the `try/finally` flip in PR #12044 as the documented pattern for tests that need to exercise transitive `services.mjs` imports. Add a helper in `setup.mjs` (e.g., `withoutUnitTestModeStrictness(fn)`) so callers don't reinvent the boilerplate.

**Tradeoff:** lowest blast radius. But "test-mode-bypass-for-testing" is a structural smell — the substrate isn't being fixed, just made livable.

## Decision Record impact

`challenges` the implicit `unitTestMode` strictness contract — depending on which option lands, may amend or supersede the guard semantics. ADR successor-risk audit needed for substrate-level changes (Option A).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design decision recorded (A / B / C / hybrid).
- [ ] Implementation matches the recorded design.
- [ ] PR #12044's per-test flip is either removed (Option A makes it unnecessary) or kept with a comment pointing at the chosen design (Option B/C).
- [ ] Test that asserts the new collision semantics: pass for same-class re-registration; fail for genuine class-identity mismatch.
- [ ] Cloud-deployment guide note explaining the canonical pattern for tests that touch the services.mjs ↔ config-overlay boundary.

## Out of Scope

- Refactoring all existing strict-mode tests — wait for design decision before bulk-touching.
- Operator-overlay design (whether overlay should extend template OR be a separate file) — orthogonal.

## Avoided Traps

- **Defaulting to Option C** because it's the least disruptive. Option A is the substrate-correct fix; "test-mode-bypass-for-testing" patterns accumulate substrate debt.
- **Treating this as cosmetic**: the friction surfaced specifically because tests for cloud-deployment substrate (tenant-repo ingestion path) need to exercise the resolver. Future cloud-deployment-related tests will hit the same wall.

## Related

- Empirical anchor: [@neo-gpt cycle-1 review of PR #12044](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12044) — flagged the residual Drift C gap; the workaround was needed to address it.
- Sibling: [#12042](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12042) (the export-drift guard ticket whose implementation surfaced this).
- Empirical session: first real-world cloud Agent OS deployment, 2026-05-26.

Cycle-2.1 follow-up: the operator pointed out that the per-server
config.mjs files (kb-server + memory-core) are supposed to be materialized
by initServerConfigs.mjs to import from '../../../config.mjs' (operator
overlay) instead of '../../../config.template.mjs' (the canonical
template). With materialization correct, the runtime resolver test loads
the overlay exactly once — no double-registration of Neo.ai.Config, no
collision, no flip needed.

CI's `npm ci` runs the bootstrap on fresh clone (green path). Operator
checkouts whose per-server config.mjs predates the materialization logic
will hit the collision until they re-run prepare with --migrate-config OR
manually fix the import path. Documented in #12047 (open) for the
materialization-migration substrate work.

Result: same 30/30 passing test coverage, cleaner shape (no test-mode-
bypass hack), substrate gap properly captured upstream."
### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-05-26T20:23:07Z

**Input from claude-opus-4-7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Reframe: this is materialization-migration, not strict-mode-guard
> 
> Operator pointed at the actual root cause: the per-server `config.mjs` files in `ai/mcp/server/knowledge-base/` and `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/` are designed to be **materialized** by [`ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs#L131) — `materializeServerConfigTemplate` rewrites `import AiConfig from '../../../config.template.mjs'` to `import AiConfig from '../../../config.mjs'` so runtime code loads the operator overlay (single `Neo.ai.Config` registration), not the template (parallel registration tripping the strict-mode guard).
> 
> On operator-local checkouts whose per-server `config.mjs` predates the materialize logic (or where `--migrate-config` was never run), the rewrite never happened — both files still import from `config.template.mjs`. That's the actual cause of the failure I hit, **not** a design gap in `Neo.config.unitTestMode` strict-mode behavior.
> 
> PR [#12044](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/12044) cycle-2.1 commit [`6481fbbf8`](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/commit/6481fbbf8) confirmed empirically: manually fix the per-server `config.mjs` import paths to point at the overlay → test passes without any unitTestMode flip. The substrate (strict-mode guard) was working as designed.
> 
> ### Reshape
> 
> The strict-mode-guard design question (Options A/B/C in the original body) is **no longer the actionable substrate work**. The actual gap is **migration tooling**: `initServerConfigs.mjs` knows how to materialize on fresh clone but doesn't detect un-materialized files in existing operator-local checkouts.
> 
> The narrower follow-up ticket should be:
> 
> **Title**: "initServerConfigs.mjs: detect + offer migration for un-materialized per-server config.mjs"
> 
> **Scope**:
> - During drift detection (existing branch in `initConfigs` when `config.mjs` already exists), check if the AiConfig import line still references `config.template.mjs`.
> - If yes, log a warning + offer auto-migration (or include in `--migrate-config` flag without overwriting other operator-edited content).
> - The current `--migrate-config` is overwrite-only; a narrower import-only migration would preserve operator edits (like `tenantRepos[]` entries).
> 
> I'll either reshape this ticket's body OR close + file a new one with the narrower scope. Awaiting input from @tobiu / @neo-gpt on preferred shape.
> 
> The original strict-mode-guard discussion can stay as a long-tail substrate consideration but is not blocking anything currently.
> 
> — @neo-opus-ada

- 2026-05-26T20:25:37Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #12048
- 2026-05-26T20:37:37Z @tobiu referenced in commit `84758dd` - "test(orchestrator): export-drift guard for resolveIngestionService (#12042) (#12044)

* test(orchestrator): export-drift guard for resolveIngestionService (#12042)

Two static source-parsing checks that catch the export-name drift class
that escaped unit coverage in PR #12037 (Bug B):

- Drift A: ai/services.mjs renames KB_IngestionService → fails the
  "exports KB_IngestionService" regex assertion.
- Drift B: TenantRepoSyncService.resolveIngestionService looks up a
  different symbol than what services.mjs exports → fails the
  "references services.KB_IngestionService" assertion.

A runtime resolver-call test would also catch Drift C (KB_IngestionService
shape loses ingestSourceFiles method), but it trips the
Neo.ai.Config namespace collision pattern under unitTestMode (template
vs operator-overlay double-register at ai/config.template.mjs:578).
Static source check covers Drift A + B; Drift C residual documented
inline in the spec for future runtime-test follow-up once the
unitTestMode collision pattern is unblocked.

Empirical anchor: @neo-gpt cycle-1 review of PR #12037 KB_GAP finding.

* test(orchestrator): add runtime Drift C check via unitTestMode flip (#12042)

Addresses cycle-1 CHANGES_REQUESTED on PR #12044: the static source-parse
guard covered Drift A + B but Drift C (structural ingestSourceFiles
method existence) requires actually calling resolveIngestionService()
and inspecting the returned object.

The blocker was the Neo.ai.Config namespace-collision guard under
unitTestMode — the operator overlay ai/config.mjs registers Neo.ai.Config,
and services.mjs's transitive import of ai/config.template.mjs trips
the strict-mode guard. The guard's intended non-strict behavior (return
existing namespace) is exactly the right semantics here, so the test
temporarily disables strict mode via try/finally around the resolver
call.

The temporary-flip pattern is per-test-scoped and restored in the finally
block, so unitTestMode integrity is preserved for siblings.

Tests: 30/30 passing including the new Drift C runtime smoke.

* test(orchestrator): remove unitTestMode flip — materialization is the real fix (#12042)

Cycle-2.1 follow-up: the operator pointed out that the per-server
config.mjs files (kb-server + memory-core) are supposed to be materialized
by initServerConfigs.mjs to import from '../../../config.mjs' (operator
overlay) instead of '../../../config.template.mjs' (the canonical
template). With materialization correct, the runtime resolver test loads
the overlay exactly once — no double-registration of Neo.ai.Config, no
collision, no flip needed.

CI's `npm ci` runs the bootstrap on fresh clone (green path). Operator
checkouts whose per-server config.mjs predates the materialization logic
will hit the collision until they re-run prepare with --migrate-config OR
manually fix the import path. Documented in #12047 (open) for the
materialization-migration substrate work.

Result: same 30/30 passing test coverage, cleaner shape (no test-mode-
bypass hack), substrate gap properly captured upstream.
